name: Clavijo and Hermitage of Saint James Loop
name_original: Valle de Iregua – circular desde Logroño por el Castillo de Clavijo y la Real Capilla de Santiago
description: This difficult 25.4-mile touring cycling route from Logroño explores the Valle de Iregua, passing Castillo de Clavijo and Real Capilla de Sa
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Logroño traverse a landscape characterized by extensive vineyards and the winding Ebro and Iregua rivers. The region features a mix of flat river paths, rolling hills, and more challenging ascents through wine country. Natural features like La Grajera Park and Reservoir offer additional cycling opportunities on varied surfaces. This diverse terrain provides options for different skill levels, from leisurely rides to more demanding excursions. Best touring cycling routes around Logroño The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de la Grajera – circular from Logroño, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route circles the La Grajera Reservoir, offering views of the water and surrounding parkland. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valle de Iregua – circular from Logroño via Castillo de Clavijo and Real Capilla de Santiago, a difficult 25.3 miles (40.8 km) path. This route explores the Iregua River valley, passing historical sites and offering varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Iregua River – Nalda Castle loop from Logroño, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail leading through the Iregua River valley, often completed in about 3 hours 16 minutes. Touring cycling around Logroño is defined by vineyard landscapes, riverside paths along the Ebro and Iregua, and views of surrounding hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging routes. The routes in Logroño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Logroño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Logroño?
A: There are over 220 touring cycling routes around Logroño, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored more than 4000 touring cycling tours in the region.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on touring cycling routes near Logroño?
A: Touring cycling routes around Logroño are primarily characterized by extensive vineyards, often with views of 'wildly romantic mountain landscapes.' You'll also find paths along the winding &lt;strong&gt;Ebro&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Iregua rivers&lt;/strong&gt;, and opportunities to cycle through natural features like &lt;strong&gt;La Grajera Park and Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;. The terrain varies from flat river paths to rolling hills and more challenging ascents through wine country.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Logroño?
A: Yes, Logroño offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. For example, the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; provides mostly paved surfaces and loops suitable for various fitness levels. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, the region has 41 easy routes out of 226 total, ensuring options for less experienced cyclists.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes around Logroño?
A: Absolutely. Logroño offers 126 difficult touring cycling routes for those seeking a challenge. A notable difficult route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994461732/valle-de-iregua-circular-from-logrono-via-castillo-de-clavijo-and-real-capilla-de-santiago" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Iregua – circular from Logroño via Castillo de Clavijo and Real Capilla de Santiago&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 25.3 miles (40.8 km) and includes significant elevation changes, exploring the Iregua River valley and historical sites.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available from Logroño?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Logroño are circular. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994350519/embalse-de-la-grajera-circular-from-logrono" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Grajera – circular from Logroño&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the La Grajera Reservoir. For a more challenging circular ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31585640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Park – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Logroño&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 90 km route.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with wine tasting in Logroño?
A: Yes, the La Rioja region is synonymous with wine, and many cycling tours are designed to visit prestigious bodegas. You can cycle past architecturally significant wineries like &lt;strong&gt;Ysios&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Marqués de Riscal&lt;/strong&gt;, or &lt;strong&gt;Bodegas Campo Viejo&lt;/strong&gt;. These visits often include wine tastings and insights into wine culture, such as at the Vivanco Museum of Wine Culture.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I visit while cycling near Logroño?
A: Logroño is a key stop on the historic &lt;strong&gt;Camino de Santiago&lt;/strong&gt;, allowing cyclists to follow segments of this ancient pilgrimage route. Routes also frequently lead to charming medieval villages like &lt;strong&gt;Laguardia&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its tunnels and caves, and other historic towns such as &lt;strong&gt;Santo Domingo de la Calzada&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Nájera&lt;/strong&gt;. Within Logroño, you can visit the &lt;strong&gt;Concatedral de Santa María de la Redonda&lt;/strong&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints accessible by touring bike around Logroño?
A: Yes, several spots offer panoramic vistas of Logroño and its surroundings. The &lt;strong&gt;Tondeluna Viewpoint&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Mecca Hill&lt;/strong&gt; are known for providing stunning photographic opportunities and unique perspectives of the city and region, and are often incorporated into cycling routes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Logroño?
A: The touring cycling routes in Logroño are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from quiet vineyard roads to riverside paths, and the well-maintained routes that cater to various ability levels.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Logroño?
A: The region's climate makes spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) ideal for touring cycling. During these seasons, the weather is generally mild, and the landscapes are particularly beautiful, with blooming vineyards in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summer can be hot, especially in July and August, while winters are cooler and may have occasional rain.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or parks to explore by bike near Logroño?
A: Yes, Logroño offers several natural attractions. &lt;strong&gt;La Grajera Park and Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt; is a significant feature with loops and trails. The &lt;strong&gt;Ebro River&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Iregua River&lt;/strong&gt; provide pleasant riverside paths. Slightly further afield, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Cebollera Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; offers lush forests and clear rivers for more adventurous cyclists.

Q: What are some notable natural monuments or landmarks along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the routes, you can encounter several natural monuments and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4509944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osborne Bull&lt;/a&gt;, the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of the Witch's Hut&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3764706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of Lapuebla de Labarca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Logroño?
A: Logroño, as a regional capital, offers various parking options within the city and at the start of popular routes. For routes around &lt;strong&gt;La Grajera Park&lt;/strong&gt; or along the &lt;strong&gt;Ebro River&lt;/strong&gt;, you can often find designated parking areas or street parking nearby. It's advisable to check local parking regulations or use public parking facilities in Logroño city center, which are well-connected to the cycling network.
tips: Baroque building from the 18th century, located on the southern slope of Monte Laturce and which is reached after a short but demanding ascent from Clavijo.
Renaissance-style temple built with stone from the region during the 16th and 16th centuries. The church has a single nave and a 26 meter high tower.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Valle de Iregua circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult and requires a very good level of fitness. Cyclists should be prepared for strenuous sections, including unpaved paths and demanding ascents, where you might need to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, covering approximately 40.8 kilometers (25.3 miles) with about 610 meters (2000 feet) of elevation gain. You'll cycle along riverside paths of the Iregua River, through rolling hills, and experience demanding climbs in the region's wine country. Be aware that while much of La Rioja is paved, this specific route includes significant unpaved sections and mountain tracks.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route blends natural beauty with historical significance. You'll pass by the impressive Clavijo Castle and the culturally important Royal Basilica of Santiago in Logroño, which features a carved depiction of the legendary Battle of Clavijo. You'll also see the Church of the Assumption of Clavijo.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While the route offers picturesque views year-round, autumn is particularly recommended. The Iregua Valley transforms into a visual delight with the changing colors of the leaves, making for a stunning cycling experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Logroño?
faqs.answer: Since the route is a circular one starting from Logroño, you can typically find parking within the city. Look for public parking areas near the Iregua River or the city center, keeping in mind that parking availability might vary.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is explicitly described as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness.' It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children due to its strenuous sections, significant elevation gain, and unpaved terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle on public trails in this region. However, always respect private property and local regulations, especially when passing through vineyards or rural areas.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor areas in La Rioja are dog-friendly, this specific route includes unpaved sections and can be quite strenuous. If you bring your dog, ensure they are fit enough for a long, difficult ride and always keep them on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and diverse terrain, pack essentials like plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate cycling gear. Consider a mountain bike or a touring bicycle with good tires suitable for unpaved sections. A helmet is essential, and layers of clothing are advisable for changing weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near towns like Clavijo and starts from Logroño, where you can find cafes and restaurants. However, it's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the more remote sections, as services might not be consistently available.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths, including the Camino de la Mora, Paseo del Iregua, and various segments of the Via Romana del Iregua. You'll also briefly cross parts of the Camino Francés - 01.
